items.title: MOH’s Proposed RM3b Budget Cut Risks Worsening Health Care Crisis: Galen Centre
items.summary: Galen Centre for Health and Social Policy CEO Azrul Mohd Khalib warned a RM3.06b MOH budget cut would mean longer waits, shortages, staff burnout, and preventable deaths. He said the cut would not even match the RM4b spent monthly on RON95 and diesel subsidies. The post MOH’s Proposed RM3b Budget Cut Risks Worsening Health Care Crisis: Galen Centre appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Ascott Makes Full Switch To Cage-Free Eggs In Malaysia Amid Cost, Supply Concerns
items.summary: Ascott has fully switched to cage-free eggs across its Malaysian properties, positioning the move as scalable despite industry concerns over cost and supply. Stakeholders say it shows that large-scale adoption is feasible and could support wider industry uptake. The post Ascott Makes Full Switch To Cage-Free Eggs In Malaysia Amid Cost, Supply Concerns appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Report: Treasury Wants RM3b Cut To Health Ministry’s 2026 Budget
items.summary: The Treasury has ordered budget cuts for various ministries this year due to a ballooning subsidy bill, specifying a proposed cut of about RM3 billion (6.6%) to the Health Ministry's RM46.5 billion 2026 budget, leaving MOH with a smaller budget than 2025. The post Report: Treasury Wants RM3b Cut To Health Ministry’s 2026 Budget appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Reintroduce Tobacco GEG Under Act 852 — MCTC
items.summary: Malaysia should follow the UK's example and reintroduce the tobacco generational end game (GEG) under the Control of Smoking Products for Public Health Act 2024 (Act 852) to protect future generations from nicotine addiction. The post Reintroduce Tobacco GEG Under Act 852 — MCTC appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: JPA Launches ‘Reform Circular’ For Civil Service Renaissance
items.summary: JPA launches a Reform Circular to create a "cultural renaissance" in the civil service, aiming to change the stereotype of the apathetic bureaucrat. Reforms involve a fundamental change in the public service mindset, going beyond digitising outdated forms. The post JPA Launches ‘Reform Circular’ For Civil Service Renaissance appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Raise ‘Pro-Poor’ Tobacco Taxes For ‘Triple-Win Policy’, Say Anti-Smoking Advocates
items.summary: Anti-tobacco groups want a big one-off hike in cigarette excise duty, followed by automatic annual tax increases. Tobacco taxes are a "pro-poor" health protection measure and a "triple-win policy" for public health, health inequity, and revenue generation. The post Raise ‘Pro-Poor’ Tobacco Taxes For ‘Triple-Win Policy’, Say Anti-Smoking Advocates appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Fifty Patients, Two Doctors: Unsafe Overcrowding Crisis in HRPB Medical Wards — Medical Officer
items.summary: Amid bed shortages in HRPB's medical wards, patients are being placed as "lodgers" in other wards. One medical team of 2 internal medicine MOs and 1-2 HOs is expected to manage 50+ patients across multiple wards in multiple locations, with multiple risks. The post Fifty Patients, Two Doctors: Unsafe Overcrowding Crisis in HRPB Medical Wards — Medical Officer appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: GP-Led Care Central To Ageing Nation Strategy — MMA
items.summary: Our seniors deserve more than fragmented solutions. They deserve a system designed around their realities, one that brings care closer to where they live. The post GP-Led Care Central To Ageing Nation Strategy — MMA appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Ampang Hospital’s Flooded Operating Theatre Resuming Operations Tomorrow
items.summary: When it rains, it pours — literally in the case of Ampang Hospital with a flooded OT, while its IT system has been down for nearly a week. The OT will resume operations tomorrow as the problem has been fixed. But the eHIS system is still being worked on. The post Ampang Hospital’s Flooded Operating Theatre Resuming Operations Tomorrow appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Nationalism In Malaysia’s Health Service — Specialist
items.summary: I had to beg for almost 2 years just to obtain an office as the neurosurgery HOD. Over the next 5 years, no additional furniture was provided. If the “atasan” were truly nationalistic, wouldn't they equip their workers with basic necessities to function? The post Nationalism In Malaysia’s Health Service — Specialist appeared first on CodeBlue .
